title: The Silmarillion
author: J. R. R. Tolkien
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Silmarillion" book by J. R. R. Tolkien?
Options:
A. Let the Orcs keep their lands, and we will keep ours. There's enough room in this world for both, if the Elves let us be! The listeners were astonished and afraid at this idea of dividing the realms. They chose to leave the Elven lands far behind. But then Amlach denied he was even there or said any of that. The Men didn't know what to think. So the Elf-friends told them: Now you must see - there is a Dark Lord with spies among us. He's afraid of the power we could give his enemies.  
B. The Orcs can have their own realm, and we will have ours. The world is large enough for both our kinds, if the Elves allow us to live in peace! But the listeners were shocked, and afraid at this idea of separation. Though they decided to move far away from the Elven lands. Later Amlach denied being part of this discussion or saying such things. Leaving the Men confused and uncertain. So the Elf-friends said: Surely you must believe now - there is a Dark Lord, whose spies are among us. For he fears our potential strength against him.
C. The Orcs can have their realm, and we will have ours. The world has space for both kinds, if the Elves allow us to exist in peace! Those listening were shocked and fearful at the idea of separation. They decided to move far from Elven lands. Later though, Amlach denied being there or saying such things. Leaving the Men confused and unsure. So the Elf-friends said: Surely now you must believe - there is a Dark Lord with spies among us. Because he fears the strength we could give his foes.
D. Let the Orcs have the realm that is theirs, and we will have ours. There is room in the world, if the Eldar will let us be!' Then those that listened sat for a while astounded, and a shadow of fear fell on their hearts; and they resolved to depart far from the lands of the Eldar. But afterwards Amlach returned among them, and denied that he had been present at their debate or had spoken such words as they reported; and there was doubt and bewilderment among Men. Then the Elf-friends said: 'You will now believe this at least: there is indeed a Dark Lord, and his spies and emissaries are among us; for he fears us, and the strength that we may give to his foes.
Answer:

options.A: Let the Orcs keep their lands, and we will keep ours. There's enough room in this world for both, if the Elves let us be! The listeners were astonished and afraid at this idea of dividing the realms. They chose to leave the Elven lands far behind. But then Amlach denied he was even there or said any of that. The Men didn't know what to think. So the Elf-friends told them: Now you must see - there is a Dark Lord with spies among us. He's afraid of the power we could give his enemies.
options.B: The Orcs can have their own realm, and we will have ours. The world is large enough for both our kinds, if the Elves allow us to live in peace! But the listeners were shocked, and afraid at this idea of separation. Though they decided to move far away from the Elven lands. Later Amlach denied being part of this discussion or saying such things. Leaving the Men confused and uncertain. So the Elf-friends said: Surely you must believe now - there is a Dark Lord, whose spies are among us. For he fears our potential strength against him.
options.C: The Orcs can have their realm, and we will have ours. The world has space for both kinds, if the Elves allow us to exist in peace! Those listening were shocked and fearful at the idea of separation. They decided to move far from Elven lands. Later though, Amlach denied being there or saying such things. Leaving the Men confused and unsure. So the Elf-friends said: Surely now you must believe - there is a Dark Lord with spies among us. Because he fears the strength we could give his foes.
options.D: Let the Orcs have the realm that is theirs, and we will have ours. There is room in the world, if the Eldar will let us be!' Then those that listened sat for a while astounded, and a shadow of fear fell on their hearts; and they resolved to depart far from the lands of the Eldar. But afterwards Amlach returned among them, and denied that he had been present at their debate or had spoken such words as they reported; and there was doubt and bewilderment among Men. Then the Elf-friends said: 'You will now believe this at least: there is indeed a Dark Lord, and his spies and emissaries are among us; for he fears us, and the strength that we may give to his foes.
